step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with agent1’s assessment. Thyroid hormones, particularly T3 and T4, play a crucial role in increasing the number and affinity of β-adrenergic receptors on cardiac cells, thereby enhancing their sensitivity to epinephrine. Additionally, these hormones elevate overall cellular metabolism, further amplifying the physiological responses mediated by catecholamines. Thus, the effect of thyroid hormones extends beyond just receptor modulation but also includes broader metabolic changes that support greater responsiveness to hormonal stimuli.

Answer: C Your agreement adds an important point about how thyroid hormones not only affect adrenergic receptors but also influence overall cellular metabolism, providing additional context for why they would enhance cardiac responsiveness to epinephrine. Given this comprehensive understanding, my final answer remains consistent with yours.
